summary: The diff demonstrates implementation of TanStack Router fixes for admin panel routing, URL parameter handling utilities, and admin access controls. However, evidence for admin link visibility in the Header component and comprehensive end-to-end testing is not fully verifiable from the diff summary.

missing_requirements.requirement: Ensure the Admin Panel navigation link in the Header component is visible for authenticated admin users in both draft and production deployments
missing_requirements.reason: The frontend-file-summaries.txt mentions the Header has a 'prominent admin panel link (visible only to authenticated admin users with blue background)', but the actual Header.tsx code is not shown in the diff to verify the implementation details or confirm it properly checks admin authentication

missing_requirements.requirement: Test the complete admin panel access flow end-to-end
missing_requirements.reason: No test files or test implementation evidence is present in the diff. REQ-51 explicitly requires end-to-end testing of the complete flow including authentication, navigation via direct URL and header link, and verification of all admin dashboard tabs

hallucinated_features.description: Comprehensive URL parameter utility functions with session storage persistence
hallucinated_features.reason: The urlParams.ts file includes extensive session storage functionality (storeSessionParameter, getSessionParameter, getPersistedUrlParameter, clearSessionParameter, clearUrlParameter) which goes beyond what was requested. The BuildRequest only asks for fixing routing to handle the caffeineAdminToken parameter, not building a general-purpose parameter persistence system

hallucinated_features.description: Backend access control modifications and admin query enhancements
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend/main.mo file shows modifications including improved error handling for useIsCallerAdmin and expanded admin query types (ProductAdminQueries, OrderAdminQueries, UserAdminQueries). The BuildRequest explicitly states as a constraint 'Do not change the backend access control logic' and lists as a non-goal 'Adding new admin features or capabilities'
